entries.title: Eccentricity of Conic
entries.content: A parameter that determines how much a conic section deviates from being a circle.
entries.example: Eccentricity of a circle is 0

entries.title: Edge of Graph
entries.content: A line segment connecting two vertices in a graph.
entries.example: Connecting node A to node B

entries.title: Echelon Form
entries.content: A matrix format where all non-zero rows are above zero rows, and leading entry of a row is to the right of the row above.
entries.example: Matrix with zeroes below main diagonal path

entries.title: Eigenvalue definition
entries.content: A scalar lambda associated with a linear transformation such that Av = lambda * v.
entries.example: Finding growth factor along principal axes

entries.title: Eigenvector equation
entries.content: The non-zero vector v that changes only by a scalar factor when a linear transformation is applied.
entries.example: Finding direction of stretch in coordinate grid

entries.title: Element of Set
entries.content: An individual object or member contained within a set.
entries.example: 3 is an element of the set of prime numbers

entries.title: Elimination Method
entries.content: A method of solving systems of equations by adding or subtracting equations to eliminate variables.
entries.example: Solving 2x + y = 5 and x - y = 1 by adding them

entries.title: Ellipse Equation
entries.content: The algebraic equation representing a squashed circular path with two focal points.
entries.example: An ellipse centered at origin with width 6 and height 4

entries.title: Empirical Rule
entries.content: In a normal distribution, nearly all data falls within 3 standard deviations of the mean (68-95-99.7).
entries.example: Predicting test scores variance in a population

entries.title: Empty Set
entries.content: The unique set containing no elements, denoted by a circle with a slash or empty braces.
entries.example: The set of integers that are both odd and even

entries.title: Endomorphism
entries.content: A homomorphism from a mathematical structure to itself.
entries.example: Linear transformation from R^2 to R^2

entries.title: Entire Function
entries.content: A complex-valued function that is holomorphic (differentiable) at all points in the complex plane.
entries.example: f(z) = e^z is an entire function

entries.title: Entropy in Information Theory
entries.content: A measure of the average amount of information or uncertainty in a random variable's outcomes.
entries.example: Measuring information density in compressed files

entries.title: Epigraph
entries.content: The set of points lying on or above the graph of a function.
entries.example: The area above a parabola y = x^2

entries.title: Epimorphism
entries.content: A surjective homomorphism between two algebraic structures.
entries.example: Mapping integers to integers modulo 5

entries.title: Equality Relation
entries.content: A binary relation asserting that two expressions represent the same mathematical object.

entries.title: Equilateral Triangle
entries.content: A triangle in which all three sides are equal in length, and all angles are 60 degrees.
entries.formula: a = b = c, A = B = C = 60 degrees
entries.example: Area = (sqrt(3)/4) * s^2

entries.title: Equivalence Class
entries.content: A subset of a set containing all elements that are equivalent to each other under an equivalence relation.
entries.example: Integers modulo 2 splits integers into evens and odds classes

entries.title: Equivalence Relation
entries.content: A binary relation that is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.
entries.formula: a~a; a~b => b~a; a~b & b~c => a~c
entries.example: Congruence modulo n on integers

entries.title: Erlang Distribution
entries.content: A continuous probability distribution with two parameters, representing the sum of k independent exponential variables.
entries.example: Modeling waiting times in telephone queuing systems

entries.title: Error Function
entries.content: A special function of sigmoid shape that occurs in probability, statistics, and partial differential equations.
entries.formula: erf(z) = (2/sqrt(pi)) * integral from 0 to z of e^(-t^2) dt
entries.example: Calculating probabilities for the standard normal distribution

entries.title: Euclidean Algorithm
entries.content: An efficient method for computing the greatest common divisor (GCD) of two integers.
entries.formula: gcd(a, b) = gcd(b, a mod b)

entries.title: Euclidean Distance
entries.content: The straight line distance between two points in Euclidean space.
entries.example: Distance between points in coordinates

entries.title: Euclidean Geometry
entries.content: The study of plane and solid figures based on the axioms and postulates of Euclid of Alexandria.
entries.example: Parallel lines never intersect in a flat plane

entries.title: Euclidean Space
entries.content: The fundamental space of classical geometry, representing physical dimensions with coordinates.
entries.example: Three-dimensional space R^3

entries.title: Euler-Mascheroni Constant
entries.content: A mathematical constant defined as the limiting difference between the harmonic series and the natural logarithm.
entries.example: gamma approximately equals 0.57721

entries.title: Euler's Constant e
entries.content: The base of natural logarithms, representing continuous growth.
entries.example: e approx 2.71828

entries.title: Euler's Formula
entries.content: A formula establishing the deep relationship between trigonometric functions and complex exponential functions.

entries.title: Euler's Method
entries.content: A first-order numerical procedure for solving ordinary differential equations with a given initial value.
entries.example: Approximating population growth step-by-step

entries.title: Euler's Totient Function
entries.content: An arithmetic function that counts the positive integers up to n that are relatively prime to n.
entries.example: phi(10) = 4 (since 1, 3, 7, 9 are coprime to 10)

entries.title: Even Function
entries.content: A function whose graph is symmetric with respect to the y-axis.
entries.example: f(x) = x^2 or f(x) = cos(x)

entries.title: Even Number
entries.content: An integer that is exactly divisible by 2.
entries.example: Numbers like -4, 0, 2, 8

entries.title: Event Probability
entries.content: The likelihood of a specific outcome or set of outcomes occurring in a random experiment.
entries.example: Probability of rolling a 6 on a six-sided die is 1/6

entries.title: Exact Differential Equation
entries.content: A first-order ordinary differential equation that is the total differential of some function.
entries.example: Solving equations by finding potential function U(x, y)

entries.title: Excircle
entries.content: An escribed circle of a triangle, which is tangent to one side and extensions of the other two sides.
entries.example: A triangle has exactly three excircles

entries.title: Existential Quantifier
entries.content: A logical symbol indicating that there exists at least one element in the domain satisfying the property.
entries.formula: exists x in S such that P(x)
entries.example: Statement: 'There exists an even prime number'

entries.title: Expected Value
entries.content: The long-run average value of repetitions of the same random experiment.
entries.example: Expected winnings from a lottery ticket

entries.title: Explicit Function
entries.content: A function in which the dependent variable is expressed directly in terms of the independent variable.

entries.title: Exponent Rules
entries.content: Properties for simplifying operations involving powers of terms.

entries.title: Exponential Growth
entries.content: Growth pattern where the rate of change is proportional to the current value, leading to rapid increase.
entries.example: Compound interest or unconstrained population growth
